statement.p: Find the domain of the function t defined by \displaystyle{t(x)=\frac{x+3}{x + 3}}

solution.p: The only potential for an invalid input value would be if the denominator of this expression works out to 0. In this problem, we should not first simplify this function by doing:
and say the domain is all real numbers. Instead we should solve x+3=0 and get x=-3, and find the domain to be {\left(-\infty ,-3\right)\cup \left(-3,\infty \right)}.
